ab: makaya ken a kivadaq tjanusun?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: makaya, selaing aken aravac.
en: B: Yes, I would love to.

ab: makya ken a kivavadaq tjanusun?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: inika makaya, kedri aku kingljan.
en: B:Don’t, I don’t know much about it.

ab: izua su zikang nu nakingcengelj? saljingan ken a sema zua a kivala tjanusun?
ch: A:你下午有空嗎?我想去拜訪你可以嗎?
en: A: Are you free this afternoon? I’d like to visit you, is that okay?

ab: ui, ku kaljava-u anga i tjumaq.
en: B: Okay, I’ll wait for you at home.

ab: izua su zikang nutiyav? uri kivadaq aken tjanusun ta milimilingan nua paiwan zuku.
ch: A:你明天有空嗎?我想請問你關於排灣族的傳說故事。
en: A: Are you free tomorrow? I’d like to ask you about the legendary story of the Paiwan people.

ab: nutiav uri vaik aken a magadu, neka ken i tjumaq lja.
ch: B:明天我要去山上，不在家喔。
en: B: I’m going to the mountains tomorrow, so I won’t be home.

ab: naselangda ken tu kemeljang sun aravac tua pakata na sepaiwan a kakudanan, saljinga ken ta kivada tjanusun.
ch: A:我聽說您對排灣族的傳統習俗非常了解，想要請教您一下。
en: A: I heard that you know a lot about the traditional customs of the Paiwan people, so I would like to ask you something.

ab: inika liyav aku kingljan, ljakua makaya ken a temevela tua ku kingljang.
ch: B:我所了解的不多，但是我會盡量回答。
en: B: I don’t know much about it, but I will try to answer as best I can.

ab: makaya ken a pakavidiu? nu tjaivililj ki tjamadjulu anga sivecik.
ch: A:請問我可以錄影嗎?這樣以後比較容易記錄。
en: A: Can I record the video? It will be easier to record in the future.

ab: ui, inika makuda.
en: B: Okay, no problem.

ab: pidanga a su cavilj?
en: A: How old are you?

ab: siva a pulu’ saka alu anga a ku cavilj.
en: B: I’m 98 years old.

ab: maqacuvucuvung anan sun aravac.
en: A: You’re so young.

ab: neka lja! vuluvulung anga ken.
en: B: No, I’m not! I’m old.

ab: pai ku kivadaqai kemasinu sun?
en: A: Where are you from?

ab: kemasi makazayazaya aken.
en: B: I’m from [Marjah].

ab: sepaiwan uta asu cekelj?
ch: A:請問您的太太也是排灣族人嗎?
en: A: Is your wife also a \"Paiwan\"?

ab: ini, sepiyuma timadju.
en: B: No, she’s a [Puyuma].

ab: pida a su kinacaqu a kai?
en: A: What languages do you speak?

ab: macaqu aken ta na seAmis a kata sepiyuma a kai.
ch: B: 我會說阿美語和卑南語。
en: B: I can speak Ami and Puyuma.

ab: napasilevan aken aravac tua ku kinivadaqan tjanusun a vuaq, pai makaya qivu anan sun tua pakata palisi nua qemalju?
ch: A:今天很高興能訪問您，請您談一下，有關狩獵的禁忌好嗎?
en: A: It’s a pleasure to interview you today, can you talk about the taboo of hunting?

ab: ui, ka maqacuvucuvung anan aken ru sa gadu aken a qemaljup.
ch: B:好的。我年輕的時候很常上山打獵。
en: B: Yes. I used to go hunting in the mountains when I was young.

ab: maljimalji, tucu a vuaq pariya angata a su tinuluan.
ch: A:謝謝，您今天所教導的真是太棒了。
en: A: Thank you, what you taught me today was really great.

ab: pariaria angata a ku qinaivuan. sinitaucikecikel nua tja sevalitan.
ch: B:我所説的都是千真萬確的，祖先所流傳的講述。
en: B: All that I say is true, the sayings of my ancestors.

ab: tja patemazau a tja mare kivavadavadaq, maljimalji a su pinasemalavan.
ch: A:今天的訪談就到這邊。非常謝謝您的分享。
en: A: That’s all for today’s interview. Thank you very much for sharing with us.

ab: aku siaya, paresinglingliti anga uta.
en: B: You’re welcome, let’s talk again.

ab: uri vaik anga ken, nakiljaljava ken nu tjaivalilj ki tjaliyav anga ku langdaing a milimilingan.
ch: A:我要離開了，期待下次聽到更多的故事。
en: A: I’m going to leave now and look forward to hearing more stories next time.

ab: ui, pai galjuanga.
en: B: Okay! Please take care.
